title: ajuga chamaecistus subsp. chamaecistus
intro: Ajuga chamaecistus subsp. chamaecistus, also known as bugle, is a perennial plant species in the mint family Lamiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. It is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Bugle can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
